name: CAMS
quality: High-quality data from both satellite observations and global atmospheric models
availability: Free and open access via the Copernicus website and CAMS platform
update_frequency: Near real-time updates, with daily, hourly, and monthly data
legal_restrictions: Open data policy under the Copernicus program
description: CAMS provides global data on air quality, greenhouse gases, aerosols, and other atmospheric composition variables, using a combination of satellite observations, ground-based measurements, and numerical weather prediction models.
variable_s: Ozone (O₃), Nitrogen Dioxide (NO₂), Carbon Monoxide (CO), Sulphur Dioxide (SO₂), Methane (CH₄), Aerosols (PM2.5, PM10), Carbon Dioxide (CO₂), Particulate Matter, and others related to atmospheric composition
spatial_coverage: Global, with detailed regional data for Europe and other specific areas
spatial_resolution: Varies from 10 km to 80 km depending on the variable and product (global and regional models)
temporal_coverage: Ongoing since 2003 (continuous data since service launch)
api_protocol: Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service API, OGC-compliant access, OpenDAP
limitations: Limited resolution for global coverage; some delay in processing real-time data
